name: Emergency CT Stroke Protocol
description: Emergency CT head for evaluation of acute ischemic stroke

findings.technique: Non-contrast CT head was performed emergently for stroke evaluation. CT angiography of head and neck was also performed.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Hyperacute Ischemia
sections.content: There is [no early ischemic change / no loss of gray-white matter differentiation / no sulcal effacement / no focal hypodensity] to suggest acute infarction.

sections.name: Hemorrhage
sections.content: There is [no intracranial hemorrhage / no hyperdensity / no hemorrhage / no SAH / no ICH] identified.

sections.name: Vessel Hyperdensity
sections.content: There is [no vessel hyperdensity / no hyperdense MCA sign / no hyperdense basilar artery] to suggest acute thrombus.

sections.name: Mass Effect
sections.content: There is [no mass effect / no midline shift / no herniation]. The ventricles are [not compressed].

sections.name: CT Angiography
sections.content: CTA demonstrates [no large vessel occlusion / no intracranial occlusion]. The [MCA / ICA / basilar / vertebral] is [patent / occluded].

sections.name: Aortic Arch
sections.content: The aortic arch and great vessels demonstrate [no dissection / no significant stenosis]. The carotid bifurcations are [patent].

impression:
1. No acute intracranial hemorrhage.
2. [No large vessel occlusion / Large vessel occlusion in the X] identified on CTA.
3. [No early ischemic changes / Findings consistent with acute ischemic infarction] in the [X] territory.
author: RadReport Templates
